meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging for Freelancers: Master the AI Client Workflow
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn a high-speed client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster and impress your clients as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging bottlenecks, adopt an AI-augmented workflow that treats code issues as system architecture problems rather than syntax errors. By integrating Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for contextual CLI-based debugging, you can automate root cause analysis and ship production-ready fixes in minutes instead of hours.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting for the deployment, and your terminal is bleeding red error logs. You’ve been staring at the same function for forty minutes, and the logic that seemed perfect at 2:00 AM now feels like a labyrinth of technical debt. This isn't just a coding issue; it’s a productivity trap that threatens your reputation and your profit margins.

But what if you stopped 'trying' to debug and started 'engineering' the solution? By shifting your workflow to include AI-native tooling, you transform from a solitary coder into a lead architect. It’s time to move past the frustration of trial-and-error and adopt a professional, repeatable system that ensures you deliver high-quality code every single time.

steps.title: Contextual Error Isolation
steps.description: Before fixing, you must isolate the failure. Use Claude Code to map the dependency graph and identify exactly where the state corruption occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided codebase and identify the root cause of the current runtime error. Focus on state mutations within the primary service layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze current directory and map dependency flow for runtime errors'

steps.title: Automated Test Synthesis
steps.description: Create a reproduction script to prove the bug exists. This prevents regressions and gives you a benchmark for your fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal reproduction script using Jest that triggers the identified bug in isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a test file that replicates the bug found in step 1'

steps.title: Architectural Refactoring
steps.description: Apply a fix that adheres to clean code principles rather than a 'quick patch' that will break next week.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the faulty module to handle edge cases gracefully. Ensure the fix follows DRY principles and maintains existing interface contracts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply refactor to fix the bug while maintaining interface integrity'

steps.title: Regression Verification
steps.description: Run the automated test suite to ensure the fix is stable and no side effects were introduced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent changes and verify that all related unit tests now pass without side effects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute full test suite and confirm zero regressions'

steps.title: Client Documentation Sync
steps.description: Document the fix in a way that provides value to the client, showing you are a proactive problem solver.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a brief technical summary of the fix, explaining the root cause and the preventative measures taken.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a concise markdown summary of the fix for the client'

pro_tips: Always commit your 'broken' state to a separate branch before letting AI touch it; you need a clean rollback point.
Use Claude Code to scan your entire repo for 'TODO' comments—often the bug is hiding in a half-finished implementation.
When explaining bugs to clients, focus on the 'business impact' of the fix rather than the technical complexity of the code.
Treat your debug logs as data; feed them directly into Antigravity to identify patterns in your own coding mistakes.
Automate your environment setup; if you have to spend more than 5 minutes configuring a project, you're losing billable hours.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your project using this workflow, integrate these steps into your standard CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is pre-verified by your AI agent.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business with a proven, AI-first engineering workflow. Don't let bugs dictate your schedule—master the tools that give you back your time and credibility. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
